operating_state: Total revenues increased 6.7% to $611.3 billion driven by comparable sales growth of 8.2% in the U.S., with Walmart U.S. achieving 7.0% growth and Sam's Club delivering 14.6% growth. Revenue expansion was fueled by higher average ticket from strong food sales and inflation impacts in merchandise categories, combined with transaction growth. International segment revenues remained flat as positive comparable sales across all markets were offset by U.K. and Japan divestitures and negative currency impact.
cost_structure: Gross profit rate declined 98 basis points to 23.5% primarily due to markdowns and merchandise mix shifts in the U.S., higher supply chain costs, and inflation-related LIFO charges at Sam's Club. Operating expenses as a percentage of net sales increased 23 basis points to 21.0%, impacted by opioid-related legal settlements and international reorganization charges, partially offset by sales growth and lower incremental COVID-19 costs.
material_events: The company completed buyout of Massmart noncontrolling interest shareholders increasing ownership to 100% and acquired Alert Innovation becoming full owner of the previously consolidated entity. Approved a new $20.0 billion share repurchase program replacing the previous program with $19.3 billion remaining authorized. Increased annual dividend to $2.28 per share for fiscal 2024 from $2.24 per share.
macro_exposures: The company faces continued uncertainty from inflationary pressures impacting merchandise costs beyond recent historical levels, requiring strategic pricing responses including absorbing cost increases rather than passing them to customers. Supply chain disruptions contributed to higher than normal inventory levels throughout the year. Macroeconomic volatility affects consumer confidence and employment trends, while currency exchange rate fluctuations negatively impacted international revenues.
forward_outlook: Management projects capital expenditures of approximately $17-18 billion for fiscal 2024, focusing on technology, supply chain, and customer-facing initiatives. The company maintains its financial framework emphasizing strong efficient growth through comparable sales increases, membership growth at Sam's Club and Walmart+, and accelerated eCommerce expansion. Strategic capital allocation continues prioritizing supply chain, omni-channel initiatives, technology and store remodels over new store openings.
uncertainty_profile: The company faces ongoing legal contingencies beyond the resolved opioid settlements with unresolved matters requiring continued monitoring. Business reorganization activities in international markets, particularly related to Flipkart and Massmart restructuring, create operational uncertainty. Pending accounting standard changes may impact future financial reporting though specific material impacts remain to be determined. Currency restrictions limit free transferability of cash held outside the U.S.
